title: Text Justification
description: Given an array of words and a width maxWidth, format the text such that each line has exactly maxWidth characters and is fully (left and right) justified. You should pack your words in a greedy approach; that is, pack as many words as you can in each line. Pad extra spaces ' ' when necessary so that each line has exactly maxWidth characters. Extra spaces between words should be distributed as evenly as possible. If the number of spaces on a line do not divide evenly between words, the empty slots on the left will be assigned more spaces than the slots on the right. For the last line of text, it should be left-justified and no extra space is inserted between words.
input_format: The first line contains a space-separated list of words. The second line contains the integer maxWidth.
output_format: A list of strings, where each string is a formatted line.

constraints.words[i]: consists of only English letters and symbols.

sample_test_cases.input:
This is an example of text justification.
16
sample_test_cases.output:
This    is    an
example  of text
justification.

sample_test_cases.input:
What must be acknowledgment shall be
16
sample_test_cases.output:
What   must   be
acknowledgment  
shall be
